person.name: Sagar Gharti
person.tagline: I build computational models for fluid flow problems.
person.summary: Highly motivated aerospace engineer and PhD candidate with experience in CFD, FEA, FSI, turbulence modeling, multiphase simulation, numerical methods, and engineering design. My work connects fluid mechanics, heat transfer, structural mechanics, and scientific computing to solve research and engineering problems.

projects.title: Nutrient Transport in Trabecular Bone
projects.subtitle: CFD model for convection-diffusion behavior in porous biological flow
projects.summary: Developing CFD-based models to describe nutrient transport behavior in interstitial fluid through porous bone-like geometries.
details: Formulated simulation workflows for convection-diffusion behavior of nutrients in porous flows.
Connected transport efficiency to flow behavior under different physical conditions.
Designed the project as a bridge between bio-fluid mechanics, transport phenomena, and computational modeling.

projects.title: Levitating Droplet in a Vertical Wind Tunnel
projects.subtitle: Multiphase VOF simulation with coaxial jets and turbulent field variation
projects.summary: Simulating a liquid droplet in a vertical wind tunnel with coaxial jets to study droplet stability and dynamics in a controlled turbulent field.
details: Built multiphase VOF models of a droplet interacting with a coaxial jet flow field.
Used the model to study droplet motion, near-exit flow behavior, and turbulence effects.
Prepared the workflow for future force tracking, particle history, and droplet control studies.

projects.title: RANS and LES Coaxial Jet Modeling
projects.subtitle: Comparing turbulence models for near-exit coaxial jet behavior
projects.summary: Implemented RANS and LES turbulence models to analyze the near-exit flow field of coaxial jets used in droplet levitation simulations.
details: Compared modeling approaches for resolving turbulent structures and practical design-level behavior.
Used parallel processing and HPC resources to run computationally intensive simulations.
Connected turbulence model selection to droplet stability and flow field prediction.

projects.title: Fluid-Structure Interaction in Bone Mechanics
projects.subtitle: Bone stress response and transport behavior under mechanical loading
projects.summary: Studying how fluid motion and structural stresses interact in bone-like systems, with relevance to osteoporosis under Earth and microgravity conditions.
details: Combined fluid mechanics and structural analysis ideas to study bone stress behavior.
Focused on how mechanical environment affects transport and stress-related indicators.
Built the project around the connection between biomechanics, CFD, and FEA.

projects.title: PID Control in CFD Models
projects.subtitle: Closed-loop control for inlet velocity regulation in simulation workflows
projects.summary: Implemented proportional-integral-derivative control logic in CFD models to regulate simulation parameters and stabilize target flow behavior.
details: Developed control logic for adapting inlet conditions during simulation runs.
Used the method to support droplet stabilization and automated multiphase workflows.
Built around practical research automation and repeatable simulation studies.

projects.title: Potential Flow Solver Development
projects.subtitle: Thin airfoil theory and source panel method in Python
projects.summary: Developed Python-based potential flow solvers and compared predicted aerodynamic behavior against XFLR5 results.
details: Implemented thin airfoil theory and source panel method concepts in Python.
Compared solver outputs against XFLR5 to validate trends and understand assumptions.
Strengthened understanding of computational aerodynamics from first principles.

projects.title: 2-D Plate Heat Transfer Solver
projects.subtitle: Finite difference heat transfer model with multiple boundary conditions
projects.summary: Created a finite-difference solver for 2-D heat transfer on a plate with different boundary condition configurations.
details: Solved heat conduction behavior using finite difference discretization.
Explored how boundary conditions change temperature distributions.
Connected numerical linear algebra with thermal analysis.

projects.title: Heat Flux Sensor Build
projects.subtitle: Experimental sensor assembly for high-temperature heat flux measurement
projects.summary: Built a heat flux sensor using thermostats on a six-millimeter aluminum cube to measure heat fluxes up to 350 °C.
details: Assembled and tested an experimental heat flux measurement setup.
Connected thermal gradients and material response to heat flux estimation.
Gained hands-on experience in sensor building and thermal experimentation.

projects.title: Industrial Structural Design and FEA
projects.subtitle: Power plant and heavy machinery structural analysis
projects.summary: Performed static and dynamic load calculations, stress analysis, and industrial structural design for power plant and heavy machinery projects.
details: Worked on thermal, wind, seismic, and machinery load calculations.
Supported structural design of pipe racks, silo tanks, cable racks, and heavy machinery buildings.
Collaborated with multinational engineering teams on projects in Asia and North America.

points: Formulation of an empirical relation to describe convection-diffusion behavior of nutrients in porous flows in interstitial fluid using CFD analysis with STAR-CCM+.
Simulation of a multiphase (VOF) model of a drop in a vertical wind tunnel with coaxial jets to study the drop dynamics in a varying turbulent field.
Implementation of RANS and LES turbulence models in the coaxial jet flow field simulation models.
Implementation of proportional-integrative-derivative (PID) control method in CFD models.
Fluid-structure analysis to study the bone stresses and their impact on osteoporosis in earth and microgravity.
Utilizing high-performance computing (HPC) and parallel processing to run the simulations more efficiently.

Taught undergraduate students to set up an experimental model and collect data in a wind tunnel as part of the experimental aerodynamics lab (compressible and incompressible flows), followed by data analysis and uncertainty analysis.
Held office hours for the undergraduate senior level incompressible and compressible flow course, along with grading.
Static and dynamic load calculations (thermal, wind, seismic) of heavy machinery structures.
Stress analysis and structural design of industrial structures and power plants, including pipe racks, silo tanks, cable racks, heavy machinery buildings, etc., using FEA.
Collaborated with multinational engineering teams like Mitsubishi Heavy Industries (MHI).
Representative projects included Hin-Kong Power Plant, Sakkaide Biomass Power Plant, Carbon Capture FEED projects, and industrial plant structures.
Major responsibilities: Structural analysis, design, load calculation, drawings, quantity estimation, site supervision, quality control, technical presentation, and report writing.

education.note: Ongoing research in CFD, bio-fluid mechanics, multiphase flow, and FSI.

education.degree: Master of Science in Aerospace Engineering
education.note: Graduate coursework and research in computational, bio-fluid and thermal-fluid sciences.

education.degree: Bachelor of Science in Civil Engineering
education.note: Foundation in mechanics, structures, hydraulics, and engineering design.

publications.title: Numerical Study of Nutrient Mixing in Trabecular Bone in Microgravity, Disuse and Normogravity
